ddls:
CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "connector_id" TEXT /* ID of the connector that generated the log entry */,
  "event" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., WARNING, INFO, ERROR) */,
  "message_data" TEXT /* Detailed message data associated with the log entry */,
  "message_event" TEXT /* Type of message event (e.g., warning, api_call, records_modified) */,
  "transformation_id" TEXT /* ID of the transformation applied to the log entry */,
  "time_stamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the log entry was generated */,
  "_fivetran_synced" TEXT /* Timestamp when the log entry was synced by Fivetran */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Log level (e.g., DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR) */,
  "connector_config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the connector configuration used for the log entry */,
  "parsed_data" TEXT /* Parsed log data in a structured format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_message_event FOREIGN KEY ("message_event") REFERENCES message_events ("message_event_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_connector_id FOREIGN KEY ("connector_id") REFERENCES connectors ("connector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_event FOREIGN KEY ("event") REFERENCES events ("event_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_transformation_id FOREIGN KEY ("transformation_id") REFERENCES transformations ("transformation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_connector_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("connector_config_id") REFERENCES connector_configs ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "connectors" (
  "connector_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each connector */,
  "connector_name" TEXT /* Name of the connector */,
  "configuration" TEXT /* Configuration settings for the connector */,
  "connector_type" TEXT /* Type of connector (e.g., API, file, database) */,
  "connector_version" TEXT /* Version of the connector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "connector_configs" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "connector_id" TEXT /* ID of the connector associated with the configuration */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration */,
  "config_settings" TEXT /* Configuration settings in JSON format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connector_configs_connector_id FOREIGN KEY ("connector_id") REFERENCES connectors ("connector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event type */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the event type (e.g., WARNING, INFO, ERROR) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event type */,
  "event_category" TEXT /* Category of the event (e.g., security, performance, error) */,
  "event_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the event (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "message_events" (
  "message_event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message event type */,
  "message_event_name" TEXT /* Name of the message event type (e.g., warning, api_call, records_modified) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the message event type */,
  "message_event_category" TEXT /* Category of the message event (e.g., warning, error, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("message_event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transformations" (
  "transformation_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each transformation */,
  "transformation_name" TEXT /* Name of the transformation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the transformation */,
  "transformation_type" TEXT /* Type of transformation (e.g., aggregation, filtering, parsing) */,
  "transformation_order" INTEGER /* Order in which transformations are applied */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transformation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, analyst) */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "user_job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the log entry */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the log entry was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_action" TEXT /* Specific action performed by the user (e.g., view, download, update) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES logs ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role associated with the permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "permission_category" TEXT /* Category of the permission (e.g., log management, user management) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date when the role was assigned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log statistic */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log entry associated with the statistic */,
  "event_count" INTEGER /* Number of events in the log entry */,
  "message_event_count" INTEGER /* Number of message events in the log entry */,
  "stat_category" TEXT /* Category of the statistic (e.g., event count, message event count) */,
  "stat_subcategory" TEXT /* Subcategory of the statistic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_statistics_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES logs ("log_id")
)
